characters.name: Caroline Bosman
characters.description: Caroline BosmanBiographical InformationTitleMrsGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationship StatusMarriedPhysical InformationHair ColourBlondeEye ColourBrownDiagnosesDiabeticFamily InformationSpouseTom BosmanAffiliationOccupationRadio HostTelevision PortrayalFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceGoing BodminDepartureActorFelicity MontaguCaroline Bosmanis a minor character ofDoc Martin, and appears in seven episodes. Another in a long line of “Doc Martin” comedic yet tragic characters, “Caroline Bosman” appeared in Seasons 1, 2, and 6 of the “Doc Martin” TV series. She was played by English actress Felicity Montagu. Introduced tothe docjust before the harbor-side "Portwenn Life Day" costume awards event (in Episode 1 of Season 1, "Going Bodmin"). Caroline is likely the primary (possibly only) local radio disk jockey and talk show host on Radio Portwenn. Martin later uses her live on-air talk show, to disastrous effect, in Episode 3 (“Sh*t Happens”) when he prematurely speculates on the likely source of a mysterious outbreak of diarrhea, striking the local inhabitants.

characters.name: Joan Norton
characters.description: Joan NortonBiographical InformationTitleMrsGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedPhysical InformationHair ColourWhiteAffiliationOccupationFarmerTelevision PortrayalFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceGoing BodminThe Wrong GoodbyeActorStephanie Cole Joan NortonwasMartin's aunt and the sister ofChristopher Ellingham, Martin's father. She and Christopher inherited Haven farm, which was left to the both of them by their uncle Dick, and Christopher gave it to Joan and her husband  as a wedding present. She and Phil also played host to Martin during his childhood holidays but that ended when Christopher discovered she was having an affair withJohn Slater, and she and Martin were separated. At some point, her husband died of Motor Neurone disease, and left Joan a widow for 25 years. In 2004, when Martin moved toPortwennto become a GP, she reunited with him. She provided Martin with emotional support and loved him like a mother. Joan continued to be very close to Martin but even she became exasperated by his extreme bluntness and apparent thoughtlessness (“I sometimes wonder if you have a sensitive bone in your body, Martin”). She lived at Haven Farm, which is organically certified, that she inherited from her Uncle Dick. The farm was left to both Joan and Christopher but Martin bought out his father by selling his flat in Kensington, London to pay for the debt that Joan owned. It was revealed she died of a heart attack in Preserve the Romance, and she never got to meet her great nephew James, at the end of Series 4, after hearing the whole situation over the phone via Pauline. She was later replaced by another character (Ruth Ellingham)

characters.name: Joe Penhale
characters.description: Joe PenhaleBiographical InformationTitleMrGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationship StatusEngagedPhysical InformationHair ColourBlackEye ColourBrownDiagnosesNarcolepsyAffiliationOccupationPolice OfficerTelevision PortrayalFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceThe Apple Doesn't FallLast Christmas in PortwennActorJohn Marquez Joe (Joseph) Penhaleis a main character inDoc Martin, and appears in 63 episodes. Joe Penhale is the local police officer in the town of Portwenn. He suffers from agoraphobia, which causes him to become nervous in crowds, and narcolepsy, which means he falls asleep at random times either when he is talking or eating. He has a weak voice, is clumsy, and often interrupts important events. As an example, during the funeral service ofMartin’s aunt, Joan Norton, while everyone was singing a hymn, Joe asked for the person who owned a navy blue Mercedes, which was parked wrongly.
